data.content: This is an MSTest project on .NET 8.0. I'll start `dotnet watch test` so tests re-run automatically whenever you save a file.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use `dotnet watch test` for hot reload. This is an MSTest project targeting .NET 8.0. I can start `dotnet watch test` in async mode so the user can see the tests re-run automatically when they make changes.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` for them.

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running and watching for file changes. There's currently a build error:

- **Line 25**: `ArgumentException` needs `using System;` at the top of `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs`

Once you fix that, you'll also see a test failure: `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` expects `15.00` but the `"express"` case returns `10.00`.

The watcher will automatically re-run tests each time you save a file. Happy iterating!
data.reasoningText:
The build failed because `ArgumentException` needs `System` namespace. But the user said they have failing tests and want hot reload to iterate - so this build error is one of the things they'll fix. The watcher is running and waiting for file changes.

Actually, the missing `using System;` is a pre-existing issue. Let me just tell the user the watcher is running and they have a build error to fix.
